summary: Validated the new POST /api/integrations/winfarm/import bridge endpoint (9 dedicated pytest cases) and re-ran the full Phase-1 regression suite (14 cases). Final score: 23/23 passed. While writing tests, uncovered and fixed a MongoDB ObjectId serialization bug in the Winfarm import handler (the customer dict was returned containing the `_id` injected by `insert_one`, causing a 500 on any code path that created a new customer). Fix: pop `_id` after insert. After fix, new-customer creation path works end-to-end.

minor.issue: FIXED during this iteration: endpoint returned 500 (ObjectId not JSON serializable) whenever a brand-new customer was created, because the inserted customer dict was returned directly without stripping mongo `_id`. Patched in server.py line ~2014 by `customer.pop('_id', None)` after `insert_one`.

Phone matching is naive: builds regex `{input_digits}$` against stored customer.phone. This only matches when the INPUT digits are a trailing substring of the stored phone; it does NOT handle inputs with country prefixes longer than the stored value (e.g. stored `3201112222` + input `+393201112222` → 12-digit input will not match 10-digit stored). Consider matching by last-N-digits intersection instead.

Endpoint reads raw Request body instead of using a Pydantic model; no OpenAPI schema is exposed; aliases (telefono/cliente/indirizzo/importo/pagamento/note/ricevuta/scontrino) are undocumented outside the docstring.
`payment_method` silently falls back to 'cash' when an invalid value (e.g. 'bonifico') is sent; no 400 is returned. Probably intentional but a debug log or validation would help.

action_items: Consider also popping `_id` from `delivery_data` after `db.deliveries.insert_one(...)` to avoid relying on the dict-comprehension filter in the return statement (currently safe but brittle).
Harden winfarm phone matching: use last-9-digits normalization on BOTH sides so country-prefixed inputs from Winfarm also hit existing customers.
Add a Pydantic model `WinfarmImportIn` with Field aliases for the Italian keys to document/validate the endpoint and improve OpenAPI.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is still 2528 lines - should be split into routers. (carried over from iteration_1)
The winfarm_import handler reads request.json() raw; this is the same pattern flagged in iteration 1 for status/confirm endpoints - standardize on Pydantic models.

`customer['phone']` for the newly-created customer ends up with the RAW user input (spaces, +39 prefix, etc.) because the endpoint stores `customer_phone` verbatim - no normalization. Stored value should be normalized digits-only (same form used for matching).

success_rate.backend: 100% (23/23 incl. Phase-1 regression + new Winfarm endpoint)

test_credentials: Pharmacy test@farmaciaprova.it / Test1234!  |  Driver luca@fattorino.it / Driver123!
seed_data_creation: Used existing seed; tests create their own deliveries/customers with TEST_WINFARM_ / WF- prefixes and clean them up via mongo delete_many in teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Winfarm bridge endpoint fully covered. File /app/backend/tests/test_winfarm_import.py is reusable as regression. Tests are isolated: they use a module-scoped cleanup fixture tracking inserted delivery_ids/customer_ids/notif refs so re-runs stay idempotent. If main agent later adds a Pydantic model or changes alias keys (telefono/cliente/indirizzo/importo/pagamento/note/ricevuta/scontrino), update the test payloads accordingly. Note: the phone-suffix test is written against the CURRENT naive regex behaviour; if phone matching is improved to normalize country prefixes, relax/expand that test.
rca of the issue: Initial failure test_new_customer_when_only_unique_name -> 500. RCA: motor's `insert_one` mutates the input dict adding the generated `ObjectId` under key `_id`. The winfarm handler returned the same dict as part of the JSON response without stripping `_id`, so FastAPI/`jsonable_encoder` raised `TypeError("'ObjectId' object is not iterable")`. Reproduction: POST /api/integrations/winfarm/import with only a never-seen `customer_name`. Mitigation applied: `customer.pop('_id', None)` immediately after `await db.customers.insert_one(customer)`. Verified by re-running the full pytest suite -> 23/23 green.
